summary: auth login --chrome requires substack.sid and treats connect.sid as optional (carried only when present), instead of requiring both.
reason: Live Substack sessions do not always set connect.sid on .substack.com — custom-domain publications carry it in the custom domain's cookie jar instead. Requiring both blocked every otherwise-valid logged-in user from authenticating at all, including the read/apex surface that needs only substack.sid.
validated_outcome: auth login --chrome succeeds with substack.sid alone; doctor reports Credentials: valid; 14/16 live read commands pass against real Substack.
